candidate_definitions.text: SOTA low-bit LLM quantization (INT8/FP8/MXFP8/INT4/MXFP4/NVFP4) & sparsity; leading model compression techniques on PyTorch, TensorFlow, and ONNX Runtime
<div align="center">
PaddleSlim is an open-source library for deep model compression and architecture search.
A SOTA quantization algorithm for high-accuracy low-bit LLM inference, seamlessly optimized for CPU/XPU/CUDA, with multi-datatype support and full compatibility with vLLM, SGLang, and Transformers.
<div align="center">
Unified Efficient Fine-Tuning of 100+ LLMs & VLMs (ACL 2024)
![# LLaMA Factory](assets/logo.png)
Accessible large language models via k-bit quantization for PyTorch.
Transformers-compatible library for applying various compression algorithms to LLMs for optimized deployment with vLLM
<div align="center">
An easy-to-use LLMs quantization package with user-friendly apis, based on GPTQ algorithm.
🚨 AutoGPTQ is unmaintained - we suggest using GPTQModel for bug fixes and new models support 🚨
🤗 Optimum Intel: Accelerate inference with Intel optimization tools
A Python package for extending the official PyTorch that can easily obtain performance on Intel platform
Intel will not provide or guarantee development of or support for this project, including but not limited to, maintenance, bug fixes, new releases or updates.
🚀 Accelerate inference and training of 🤗 Transformers, Diffusers, TIMM and Sentence Transformers with easy to use hardware optimization tools
PyTorch native quantization and sparsity for training and inference
<div align="center">
FP16xINT4 LLM inference kernel that can achieve near-ideal ~4x speedups up to medium batchsizes of 16-32 tokens.
<div align="center">
LLM model quantization (compression) toolkit with HW acceleration support for Nvidia, AMD, Intel GPU and Intel/AMD/Apple CPU via HF, vLLM, and SGLang.

OpenMMLab Model Compression Toolbox and Benchmark.
<div align="center">
INT4/INT5/INT8 and FP16 inference on CPU for RWKV language model
This is a port of [BlinkDL/RWKV-LM](https://github.com/BlinkDL/RWKV-LM) to [ggerganov/ggml](https://github.com/ggerganov/ggml).
Palette quantization library that powers pngquant and other PNG optimizers
Imagequant library converts RGBA images to palette-based 8-bit indexed images, *including* alpha component. It's ideal for generating tiny PNG images and [nice-looking GIFs](https://gif.ski).
Neural Network Compression Framework for enhanced OpenVINO™ inference
<div align="center">
TinyChatEngine: On-Device LLM Inference Library

This is the official implementation of TinyEngine, a memory-efficient and high-performance neural network library for Microcontrollers.

An official lightweight library for the RaBitQ algorithm and its applications in vector search.
> **New:** We have recently open-sourced the GPU implementation of RaBitQ for high-dimensional vector search. See [cuvs_rabitq](https://github.com/Stardust-SJF/cuvs_rabitq/tree/cuvs_ivf_rabitq).

A list of papers, docs, codes about model quantization. This repo is aimed to provide the info for model quantization research, we are continuously improving the project. Welcome to PR the works (papers, repositories) that are missed by the repo.

This repo collects papers, documents, and codes about model quantization for anyone who wants to research it. We are continuously improving the project. Welcome to PR the works (papers, repositories) that the repo misses.
Quartet II Official Code
This is the official code for the Quartet II NVFP4 training paper [![arXiv](https://img.shields.io/badge/arXiv-2601.22813-b31b1b.svg)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.22813)
[ICML 2024] BiLLM: Pushing the Limit of Post-Training Quantization for LLMs
![intuition](imgs/author.png)
Q-GaLore: Quantized GaLore with INT4 Projection and Layer-Adaptive Low-Rank Gradients.

This repo contains the pre-release version of Q-GaLore algorithm, proposed by [Q-GaLore: Quantized GaLore with INT4 Projection and Layer-Adaptive Low-Rank Gradients](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.08296).
Quantization library for PyTorch. Support low-precision and mixed-precision quantization, with hardware implementation through TVM.

BitPack is a practical tool to efficiently save ultra-low precision/mixed-precision quantized models.
BitPack is a practical tool that can efficiently save quantized neural network models with mixed bitwidth.
